recipes.title: Tonkotsu Ramen
recipes.description: Rich, milky pork bone broth ramen topped with chashu, soft-boiled egg, scallions and nori.
steps: Blanch pork bones in boiling water for 10 minutes, drain and rinse thoroughly.
Simmer bones in fresh water with crushed garlic and ginger for at least 8 hours, replenishing water as needed.
Roll the pork belly tightly, tie with string and braise in soy sauce and mirin for 90 minutes to make chashu.
Soft-boil eggs for 6 minutes 30 seconds, peel and marinate in soy sauce mixture for 1 hour.
Cook ramen noodles in boiling water for 90 seconds, drain and divide into bowls.
Pour strained tonkotsu broth over noodles, top with sliced chashu, halved egg, scallions and nori.

recipes.title: Tempura
recipes.description: Light, crispy battered shrimp and assorted vegetables served with tentsuyu dipping sauce.
steps: Devein and butterfly the shrimp, making small cuts on the underside to prevent curling.
Slice sweet potato and eggplant into thin pieces, trim shiitake stems.
Whisk egg yolk with ice water, then gently fold in flour leaving lumps for a light batter.
Heat oil to 175C in a deep pan.
Dip ingredients in batter and fry in small batches for 2-3 minutes until pale golden.
Drain on a wire rack and serve immediately with tentsuyu made by simmering dashi, soy sauce and mirin.

recipes.title: Tonkatsu
recipes.description: Panko-breaded deep-fried pork cutlet served with shredded cabbage and tangy tonkatsu sauce.
steps: Score the fat edges of pork chops and pound lightly to even thickness.
Season pork with salt and pepper on both sides.
Dredge each chop in flour, dip in beaten egg, then coat thoroughly in panko.
Heat oil to 170C and fry cutlets for 6-8 minutes, turning once, until deep golden.
Rest on a wire rack for 3 minutes, then slice into strips.
Serve over finely shredded cabbage with tonkatsu sauce drizzled on top.

recipes.title: Okonomiyaki
recipes.description: Savoury Osaka-style cabbage pancake topped with sauce, mayo, bonito flakes and aonori.
steps: Finely shred cabbage and chop scallions.
Whisk flour with dashi until smooth, then add eggs and combine.
Fold in cabbage and scallions until evenly coated in batter.
Heat a non-stick pan, lay pork belly slices and ladle batter over them in a thick disc.
Cook 5 minutes, flip carefully and cook another 5 minutes until cooked through.
Top with okonomiyaki sauce, mayonnaise zigzag, bonito flakes and aonori.

recipes.title: Yakitori
recipes.description: Charcoal-grilled chicken skewers basted with sweet-savoury tare sauce.
steps: Soak bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes.
Cut chicken thighs into 2 cm cubes and slice scallions into 3 cm pieces.
Thread chicken and scallions alternately onto skewers.
Simmer soy sauce, mirin, sake and sugar until reduced by a third to make tare.
Grill skewers over hot charcoal for 4 minutes per side.
Brush generously with tare during the final minute and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Miso Soup
recipes.description: Classic Japanese soup of dashi, miso paste, tofu and wakame seaweed.
steps: Bring dashi to a gentle simmer in a saucepan.
Rehydrate wakame in cold water for 5 minutes, then drain.
Cube tofu into small pieces and add to the dashi with the wakame.
Reduce heat to low, then whisk miso paste through a fine strainer into the broth.
Heat through without boiling and finish with sliced scallions.

recipes.title: Onigiri
recipes.description: Hand-shaped salted rice balls wrapped in nori, often filled with umeboshi or salmon.
steps: Rinse rice until water runs clear, then cook with measured water in a rice cooker.
Grill the salted salmon until cooked through, then flake into pieces.
Wet hands with water and rub with a pinch of salt.
Scoop a handful of rice, press a piece of salmon or one umeboshi into the centre.
Form firmly into a triangle, pressing the edges to seal the filling inside.
Wrap a strip of nori around the base just before serving.

recipes.title: Chawanmushi
recipes.description: Silky steamed savoury egg custard with chicken, shrimp and mushrooms in dashi.
steps: Beat eggs gently without creating foam, then mix with cooled dashi, soy sauce, mirin and salt.
Strain the egg mixture through a fine sieve for the smoothest texture.
Place small pieces of chicken, shrimp and mushroom in 4 cups.
Pour egg mixture over the ingredients and skim any bubbles from the surface.
Cover cups with foil and steam over low heat for 12-15 minutes until just set.
Garnish with mitsuba and serve warm.

recipes.title: Sukiyaki
recipes.description: Tabletop hot pot of thinly sliced beef simmered in a sweet soy broth with vegetables and tofu.
steps: Combine soy sauce, mirin and sugar to make warishita sauce.
Heat a sukiyaki pan and lightly grease with beef fat trimmings.
Sear a few slices of beef quickly, then add a portion of warishita.
Add cabbage, leek, mushrooms, tofu and shirataki, simmering gently.
Continue adding ingredients gradually, replenishing sauce as needed.
Each diner dips cooked pieces in a bowl of beaten raw egg before eating.

recipes.title: Gyudon
recipes.description: Beef and onion bowl simmered in sweet soy dashi served over steamed rice.
steps: Slice the onion thinly along the grain.
Combine dashi, soy sauce, mirin, sake and sugar in a wide pan and bring to a simmer.
Add the onions and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the beef in a single layer and simmer for 5-7 minutes, skimming foam.
Spoon beef and sauce over hot rice and top with pickled red ginger.

recipes.title: Katsudon
recipes.description: Tonkatsu simmered briefly with onion and beaten egg, served over rice.
steps: Slice tonkatsu cutlets into strips.
Slice onion thinly and place in a small skillet with dashi, soy sauce, mirin and sugar.
Simmer onions for 4 minutes until tender.
Lay tonkatsu strips over the onions and pour beaten eggs around them.
Cover and cook 1-2 minutes until eggs are just set but still soft.
Slide onto a bowl of hot rice and garnish with scallions.

recipes.title: Oyakodon
recipes.description: Chicken and egg simmered together over rice in a sweet dashi sauce.
steps: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces and slice the onion thinly.
Combine dashi, soy sauce, mirin and sugar in a small skillet and bring to a simmer.
Add the onions and cook for 3 minutes.
Add chicken and simmer for 5 minutes until cooked through.
Pour beaten eggs over the chicken in a circular motion, cover and cook 1 minute.
Slide gently onto bowls of hot rice and top with mitsuba.

recipes.title: Karaage
recipes.description: Marinated Japanese fried chicken with crisp potato starch coating.
steps: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces.
Marinate chicken in soy sauce, sake, mirin, grated ginger and crushed garlic for 30 minutes.
Drain chicken and dredge thoroughly in potato starch.
Heat oil to 160C and fry chicken for 90 seconds, then remove.
Increase oil to 180C and fry a second time for 90 seconds until deep golden.
Drain and serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Takoyaki
recipes.description: Round Osaka snack of wheat batter filled with octopus, topped with sauce and bonito.
steps: Whisk flour, dashi and eggs into a thin batter.
Heat and oil a takoyaki pan thoroughly.
Pour batter to overflow each well, drop in a cube of octopus, scallion, ginger and tenkasu.
After 2 minutes, use a pick to turn each ball 90 degrees, tucking in the overflow.
Continue rotating every minute until balls are spherical and golden.
Drizzle with takoyaki sauce and mayo, top with bonito flakes.

recipes.title: Soba
recipes.description: Cold buckwheat noodles served with tsuyu dipping sauce, scallions and wasabi.
steps: Combine dashi, soy sauce and mirin and bring to a boil, then cool to make tsuyu.
Cook soba in plenty of boiling water for 4-5 minutes until just tender.
Drain and rinse under cold running water, rubbing to remove starch.
Drain thoroughly and arrange noodles on a bamboo mat or plate.
Top with shredded nori and serve with cups of cold tsuyu, sliced scallions and wasabi.

recipes.title: Udon
recipes.description: Thick wheat noodles in a hot dashi broth topped with scallions and tempura crumbs.
steps: Bring dashi to a simmer with soy sauce, mirin and salt to make kake-jiru broth.
Cook udon according to package instructions, then drain.
Divide noodles among bowls and pour hot broth over them.
Top with sliced scallions, tenkasu and slices of kamaboko.

recipes.title: Tamagoyaki
recipes.description: Layered Japanese rolled omelette, lightly sweetened and seasoned with dashi.
steps: Whisk eggs gently with dashi, sugar, soy sauce, mirin and salt.
Heat a rectangular tamagoyaki pan over medium heat and lightly oil.
Pour a thin layer of egg, pop any bubbles, and once nearly set roll it toward you.
Push the roll to the back of the pan, oil again and pour another thin layer.
Lift the existing roll so the new layer flows underneath, then roll again.
Repeat until all egg is used, shape on a bamboo mat and slice into pieces.

recipes.title: Gyoza
recipes.description: Pan-fried pork and cabbage dumplings with crispy bottoms and steamed tops.
steps: Salt the chopped cabbage, rest 10 minutes, then squeeze out water.
Mix pork with cabbage, chopped chives, grated garlic, ginger, soy sauce and sesame oil.
Place a teaspoon of filling on each wrapper, wet the edge and pleat into half-moons.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan and arrange dumplings flat-side down.
Fry 2 minutes until bases are golden, then add water and cover immediately.
Steam 6 minutes until water evaporates and bottoms are crisp.

recipes.title: Yakisoba
recipes.description: Stir-fried wheat noodles with pork, cabbage and tangy yakisoba sauce.
steps: Loosen the yakisoba noodles under warm water and drain.
Slice pork into bite-sized pieces, shred cabbage, julienne carrot and slice onion.
Heat oil in a wok and stir-fry pork until cooked through.
Add onion, carrot and cabbage and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add noodles and yakisoba sauce and toss for 2 minutes until coated and hot.
Top with pickled red ginger and aonori before serving.

recipes.title: Curry Rice
recipes.description: Japanese-style thick curry with beef, potatoes and carrots served over rice.
steps: Cube beef and chunk vegetables.
Heat oil in a heavy pot and brown the beef on all sides.
Add onions and cook until translucent, then add carrots and potatoes.
Pour in water, bring to a boil and skim foam.
Simmer covered for 30 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Turn off heat, break in curry roux pieces and stir to dissolve, then simmer 5 more minutes.
Serve over rice with fukujinzuke pickles.

recipes.title: Nikujaga
recipes.description: Homestyle Japanese stew of beef, potatoes and onions in sweet soy dashi.
steps: Cut potatoes into wedges, onions into thick slices and carrots into chunks.
Heat oil in a pot and stir-fry beef briefly until colour changes.
Add onions, potatoes and carrots and stir to coat.
Pour in dashi, sake, sugar, mirin and soy sauce.
Cover with a drop lid and simmer 20-25 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Stir in blanched snow peas at the end.

recipes.title: Teriyaki Chicken
recipes.description: Pan-seared chicken thighs glazed with a glossy sweet soy teriyaki sauce.
steps: Pat chicken dry and prick the skin with a fork.
Heat oil in a skillet and place chicken skin-side down, weighting to keep flat.
Cook 6 minutes until skin is crisp, then flip and cook 4 more minutes.
Drain excess oil and add soy sauce, mirin, sake and sugar.
Reduce sauce, basting chicken until it forms a glossy glaze.
Slice and top with sesame seeds and scallions.

recipes.title: Shoyu Ramen
recipes.description: Tokyo-style soy sauce ramen with chicken broth, chashu, bamboo shoots and egg.
steps: Simmer chicken bones with kombu in water for 4 hours, then strain.
Braise pork belly in soy sauce, mirin and sake for 90 minutes for chashu.
Reserve 200 ml of the chashu liquid as shoyu tare.
Soft-boil and marinate eggs in shoyu tare for 1 hour.
Cook noodles in boiling water for 90 seconds.
Place 3 tablespoons of tare in each bowl, add hot broth, then noodles.
Top with sliced chashu, bamboo shoots, halved egg, scallions and nori.

recipes.title: Saba Shioyaki
recipes.description: Salt-grilled mackerel with crisp skin, served with grated daikon and lemon.
steps: Score the skin of each mackerel fillet in a shallow cross pattern.
Sprinkle generously with salt and let sit for 20 minutes.
Pat dry and brush lightly with sake.
Grill skin-side up for 4 minutes, then flip and grill 5 minutes more until skin is blistered.
Serve with grated daikon, a lemon wedge and a few drops of soy sauce.

recipes.title: Hiyayakko
recipes.description: Chilled silken tofu topped with bonito, scallions, ginger and soy sauce.
steps: Drain the tofu and cut into 4 blocks.
Place each block on a small plate.
Top with finely chopped scallions and grated ginger.
Sprinkle bonito flakes generously on top.
Drizzle with soy sauce just before serving.
